title: T-009 — API: Item create (POST /api/technical/items) with V-TEC-01..04 validation
prompt:
# T-009 — API: Item create (POST /api/technical/items) with V-TEC-01..04 validation

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §6.2, §6.6, §3
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Item create endpoint with full Zod validation per V-TEC-01..04 and RBAC.

Zod schema lives in lib/technical/items/schemas.ts. Extension fields validated against reference_schemas (02-SETTINGS) — fetch active schema for items at request time and merge into Zod object. RBAC enforced via permission resolver checking technical.items.create.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/items/schemas.ts [create]
- lib/technical/items/service.ts [create]
- tests/api/items-create.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Bulk import CSV (separate spec-driven UI task T-085)
- D365 push

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given duplicate item_code for same org, when POST runs, then 409 returned with V-TEC-01 error code.
2. Given weight_mode='catch' missing nominal_weight, when POST runs, then 422 with V-TEC-02 error.
3. Given item_type='intermediate' with item_code='RM1234', when POST runs, then 422 V-TEC-03 violation.
4. Given user lacks technical.items.create, when POST runs, then 403 returned.

## Test strategy
- RED tests covering each V-TEC-01..04 violation path.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/items-create.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not perform any D365 pull/push side effects in this endpoint.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
